claims.claim: Nonprofit websites must serve two distinct audiences simultaneously — service users and donors — requiring equal visual weight for both conversion paths
Emotional storytelling and human narratives consistently outperform statistics-only messaging in nonprofit fundraising
Donation CTAs placed before earning emotional permission fail to convert; sequencing mission → science → human stories → giving produces better results
Tangible donor recognition programs (physical artifacts) lower psychological barriers to giving by converting abstract transactions into lasting representations
Grant fulfillment can be partially automated by branching on grant type (vendor-fulfilled vs. direct-payment grants)
Manual fund-balance lookups in grant approval workflows can be eliminated through API integration with database platforms
Invoice line-item structure for grant reimbursement must strictly adhere to original grant budget categories to satisfy grant administrator compliance requirements
Payment method breadth is a consistent requirement across nonprofit donation campaigns

patterns.pattern: Equine-assisted therapy nonprofits (Three Gaits, Hearts & Horses) share structurally similar audiences and benefit from identical emotional storytelling and dual-conversion-path architectures
Nonprofit grant programs with distributed member networks (lodges, chapters) require automated eligibility verification and per-entity login systems to reduce administrative overhead
Nonprofit websites frequently fail to clearly communicate core services and value proposition, causing visitor drop-off without understanding engagement pathways
Capital campaign landing pages require investment breakdowns (itemized cost allocation) rather than lump-sum goals to improve donor comprehension and confidence
Nonprofit donation pages should present payment methods in order of accessibility and friction, starting with lowest-barrier options

exceptions.general_rule: Grant reimbursement invoices should reflect actual service delivery across multiple line items
exceptions.exception: Doudlah Farms invoices must preserve original total across restructured line items, adjusting commission bases to maintain sum equivalence rather than reflecting actual sales proportions
exceptions.context: Grant compliance with Stewards Unlimited administrator requirements

exceptions.general_rule: Nonprofit websites should prioritize service-user pathways for organizations primarily serving beneficiaries
exceptions.exception: Dual-conversion architecture (equal weight to service and donor paths) applies even when one audience is significantly smaller, because both must be served simultaneously
exceptions.context: Three Gaits and Hearts & Horses equine therapy models

evidence.metric: Recognition Wall tile cost: ~$10 per tile; minimum donation threshold: $50
evidence.context: Tile cost represents 20% of minimum donation, leaving 80% directed to facility goal

evidence.metric: Three Gaits capital campaign goal: $4 million
evidence.context: Allocation: heated indoor arena, modern stable/barn, therapy & classroom space, administrative/contingency

evidence.metric: Wisconsin Masonic Foundation grant limit: $4,000 per lodge per fiscal year (May 1–April 30)
evidence.context: Approval rule enforced through Ninox database; proposed automation via API integration

evidence.metric: Doudlah Farms January 2026 invoice: $8,496 total ($4,000 retainer + $2,485 Amazon popcorn commission + $2,011 other sales commission)
evidence.context: Popcorn sales base: $62,130 (corrected from initial ~$64k after filtering non-popcorn SKUs)

evidence.metric: WP Charitable plugin cost: $99/year; expected duration: 1–2 years until facility funding goal reached
evidence.context: Enables subscription/recurring donations not available in existing WooCommerce setup

evidence.metric: Wisconsin Masonic Foundation staffing: 1.5 FTE (Adam Rigden 50%, Erica 50%, Christina 50%)
evidence.context: Manual grant approval and payment processing creates significant bottleneck
